title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a woman giving information about a weekend art workshop.
audio_script: Woman: Good afternoon, everyone, and welcome to the weekend art workshop. Before you collect your materials, I need to give you some information about the two days. Today your teacher is Mr Green, who usually teaches painting at the college. Tomorrow's class is with Anna [14]Davis[/14], who works with clay and small objects, so please be ready to try something different. The building opens at nine, but the first activity begins at [15]ten fifteen[/15]. Please do not arrive too late, because Anna will explain how to use the equipment safely. You do not need to bring paint, paper, or brushes, as we provide everything. However, please bring an old [16]shirt[/16] to protect your clothes. Lunch is not included in the price. There is a café opposite the college, but it is often busy, so we have arranged for you to eat in the [17]garden[/17] if the weather stays dry. In the afternoon, we will look at work by local artists. You can see their pictures in the small [18]gallery[/18] beside the main office. Finally, if you want to take your finished work home, please pay [19]two[/19] pounds for a strong cardboard box. We can take card payments at reception. Now, let's begin. Please keep your name label on your table, as some students will move rooms after lunch. If you finish early, you can look through the books near the window, but please do not start another activity until a teacher tells you to. We want everyone to work at a relaxed speed. Please check the notice board before you leave, because small changes to rooms or times may be written there. If you are not sure about anything, ask a member of staff.

questions.explanation: The speaker says tomorrow's class is with Anna Davis.
The speaker says the first activity begins at ten fifteen.
The speaker asks students to bring an old shirt to protect their clothes.
The speaker says they have arranged for people to eat in the garden.
The speaker says the pictures are in the small gallery.
The speaker says the cardboard box costs two pounds.
